arxiv_papers.title: Circular Raman responses from angular-momentum inequivalence in CoSi
arxiv_papers.snippet: Circularly polarized Raman scattering in solids exhibits distinct phenomena such as Raman optical activity (ROA) and chiral-phonon-induced frequency splitting, whose relationship has remained unclear. Here we show that these seemingly different responses can be understood within a common framework b

arxiv_papers.title: Electromagnetic Characterization of Magnetic Ring: Case of Circular Cross-Section Shape
arxiv_papers.snippet: This paper introduces a comprehensive two-dimensional analytical model of a toroidal magnetic ring with circular cross-section under sinusoidal excitation. Applying Maxwell's equations in local polar coordinates within a complex permeability, the model derives analytical expressions for the internal

youtube_items.summary: In 2017, I was approached by a team of designers to help finance a project they were doing here in Greece. The aim was to reuse old fishing nets and turn them into filament for 3D and turn them into filament for 3D printers. I thought the idea of recycling marine plastic waste into something workable was absolutely fantastic. So, I started working in my mind the idea of how this could actually be idea of how this could actually be reused. We put together the best minds in the fields of marine plastic waste, new technologies, and circular economy and dove right in.
